comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc*, *kolyshkin*, *zhangguanzhang* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,zhangguanzhang] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Nice catches. I don't think I've ever seen that timing flake in CI, possibly because the CI VMs are too slow.

As a hint for future reference, `/proc/uptime` has sub-second resolution and works on alpine. Your solution is more readable.

/lgtm

> I don't think I've ever seen that timing flake in CI, possibly because the CI VMs are too slow.

For the reference, it was found in containers/crun CI (running in GHA): https://github.com/containers/crun/runs/4713861436?check_suite_focus=true
